title: Spell Effects and Targeting
description: Rules for spell effects, targeting, and interaction mechanics

targeting.description: Rules for selecting and affecting targets with spells

requirements.clear_path: To target something with a spell, a caster must have a clear path to it, so it can't be behind Total Cover
requirements.self_targeting: If a spell targets a creature of your choice, you can choose yourself unless the creature must be Hostile or specifically a creature other than you

areas_of_effect.description: Some spells cover an area rather than specific targets

shapes.name: Cone
shapes.description: Area extends in a cone from the caster

shapes.name: Emanation
shapes.description: Area radiating outward from a point

shapes.name: Line
shapes.description: Straight line area from the caster

awareness.perceptible_effects: Unless a spell has a perceptible effect, a creature doesn't know it was targeted
awareness.subtle_effects: More subtle effects, such as reading thoughts, go unnoticed unless the spell says otherwise

invalid_targets.mechanics: If you cast a spell on someone or something that can't be affected by it, nothing happens to that target
invalid_targets.slot_expenditure: If you used a spell slot to cast the spell, the slot is still expended

saving_throw_appearance.success_spells: If the spell normally has no effect on a successful save, the invalid target appears to have succeeded
saving_throw_appearance.no_hint: This gives no hint that the creature is an invalid target
saving_throw_appearance.other_spells: Otherwise, you perceive that the spell did nothing to the target

effects.description: The specific outcomes of casting a spell

authority.spell_description: Effects are detailed in the spell's description and present exactly what the spell does
authority.physics_override: Spell effects ignore mundane physical laws
authority.dm_purview: Any outcomes beyond the stated effects are under the DM's purview

saving_throws.purpose: Many spells specify that a target makes a saving throw to avoid some or all effects

mechanics.ability_specified: The spell specifies which ability the target uses for the save
mechanics.outcomes: The spell describes what happens on success or failure
mechanics.dc_calculation: Spell save DC = 8 + your spellcasting ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus

attack_rolls.purpose: Some spells require the caster to make an attack roll to determine whether the spell hits a target
attack_rolls.calculation: Spell attack modifier = your spellcasting ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus
attack_rolls.resolution: Follow normal attack roll procedures for hit/miss determination

different_spells.rule: The effects of different spells add together while their durations overlap
different_spells.example: A character affected by both Bless and Guidance gains both benefits

same_spell.rule: The effects of the same spell cast multiple times don't combine
same_spell.resolution: Only the most potent effect applies while durations overlap
same_spell.tie_breaker: If castings are equally potent, the most recent effect applies
same_spell.example: Two Clerics casting Bless on the same target - the target gains the benefit only once

ongoing_spells.requirements: Spell must have ongoing duration with observable effects
ongoing_spells.limitation: Cannot identify instantaneous spells after they've been cast
